intro: Spermacoce thymoidea, also known as Thymophylla serrulata, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce thymoidea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as to promote wound healing.
